2026-04-07T05:55:52.4015000Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T05:53:48.311724591Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57852,\"title\":\"[CosmosDB for PostgreSQL] Docs: Add service-specific examples and update CHANGELOG after MPG migration\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57114 by `@haiyuazhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57114 migrated the `Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql` package from AutoRest/Swagger to the new TypeSpec-based management plane generator (MPG). A `tsp-location.yaml` was added pointing to the PostgresqlHsc TypeSpec spec. The `src/Generated/` directory was regenerated from TypeSpec.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG.md** — The `1.1.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` section is completely empty. It should describe the MPG migration (switch to TypeSpec-based generation) and any behavioral or surface changes it introduced.\\n- **README.md — Examples section** — The Examples section only contains a generic link to `(aka.ms/redacted) with no service-specific code showing how to create, update, or delete Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL clusters or configurations.\\n- **README.md — Key concepts section** — The Key concepts section just links to general Azure SDK docs instead of describing the key resources in this package: clusters, server configurations, databases, roles, firewall rules, and private link resources.\\n- **No samples directory** — There is no `tests/Samples/` directory with snippet-backed code samples illustrating common usage patterns.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the `1.1.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` section to describe the MPG migration:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.1.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n\\n- Migrated code generation from AutoRest to the new TypeSpec-based management plane generator (MPG). The public API surface is unchanged; this is an internal tooling migration.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the Key concepts section from the generic SDK link to:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql` enables you to manage the following Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL resource types:\\n\\n- **Cluster** (`CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource`) — the top-level distributed PostgreSQL cluster, which contains coordinator and worker nodes.\\n- **Server** (`CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterServerResource`) — an individual node (coordinator or worker) within a cluster.\\n- **Configuration** (`CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterConfigurationResource`, `CosmosDBForPostgreSqlServerConfigurationResource`) — PostgreSQL GUC parameters that can be set at the cluster or server level.\\n- **Database** (`CosmosDBForPostgreSqlDatabaseResource`) — a PostgreSQL database hosted on the cluster coordinator.\\n- **Role** (`CosmosDBForPostgreSqlRoleResource`) — a PostgreSQL login role.\\n- **Firewall rule** (`CosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRuleResource`) — an IP-range rule allowing external access to the cluster.\\n- **Private link resource** (`CosmosDBForPostgreSqlPrivateLinkResource`) — details about private endpoint connections.\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nReplace the Examples section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following examples show common operations. Replace ``, ``, and `` with your own values.\\n\\n### Create a Cluster\\n\\n```\\nArmClient armClient = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\nSubscriptionResource subscription = await armClient.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\nResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\nCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterCollection clusters = resourceGroup.GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters();\\n\\nCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterData clusterData = new CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterData(AzureLocation.EastUS)\\n{\\n    CoordinatorVCores = 4,\\n    CoordinatorStorageQuotaInMb = 524288,\\n    NodeCount = 2,\\n    NodeVCores = 4,\\n    NodeStorageQuotaInMb = 524288,\\n    AdministratorLoginPassword = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n};\\n\\nArmOperation lro = await clusters.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n    WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, clusterData);\\nCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster = lro.Value;\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Cluster ID: {cluster.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n### List Clusters in a Resource Group\\n\\n```\\nArmClient armClient = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\nSubscriptionResource subscription = await armClient.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\nResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\nawait foreach (CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster in resourceGroup.GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters())\\n{\\n    Console.WriteLine(cluster.Data.Name);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Add a Firewall Rule\\n\\n```\\nArmClient armClient = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\nSubscriptionResource subscription = await armClient.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\nResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster = await resourceGroup\\n    .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters()\\n    .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\nCosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRuleData firewallData = new CosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRuleData(\\n    IPAddress.Parse(\\u0026#34;0.0.0.0\\u0026#34;), IPAddress.Parse(\\u0026#34;255.255.255.255\\u0026#34;));\\n\\nArmOperation lro = await cluster\\n    .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRules()\\n    .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;AllowAll\\u0026#34;, firewallData);\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Firewall rule created: {lro.Value.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new test file at A `tsp-location.yaml` was added pointing to the PostgresqlHsc TypeSpec spec. The `src/Generated/` directory was regenerated from TypeSpec.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG.md** — The `1.1.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` section is completely empty. It should describe the MPG migration (switch to TypeSpec-based generation) and any behavioral or surface changes it introduced.\\n- **README.md — Examples section** — The Examples section only contains a generic link to `(aka.ms/redacted) with no service-specific code showing how to create, update, or delete Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL clusters or configurations.\\n- **README.md — Key concepts section** — The Key concepts section just links to general Azure SDK docs instead of describing the key resources in this package: clusters, server configurations, databases, roles, firewall rules, and private link resources.\\n- **No samples directory** — There is no `tests/Samples/` directory with snippet-backed code samples illustrating common usage patterns.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the `1.1.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` section to describe the MPG migration:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.1.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n\\n- Migrated code generation from AutoRest to the new TypeSpec-based management plane generator (MPG). The public API surface is unchanged; this is an internal tooling migration.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the Key concepts section from the generic SDK link to:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql` enables you to manage the following Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL resource types:\\n\\n- **Cluster** (`CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource`) — the top-level distributed PostgreSQL cluster, which contains coordinator and worker nodes.\\n- **Server** (`CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterServerResource`) — an individual node (coordinator or worker) within a cluster.\\n- **Configuration** (`CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterConfigurationResource`, `CosmosDBForPostgreSqlServerConfigurationResource`) — PostgreSQL GUC parameters that can be set at the cluster or server level.\\n- **Database** (`CosmosDBForPostgreSqlDatabaseResource`) — a PostgreSQL database hosted on the cluster coordinator.\\n- **Role** (`CosmosDBForPostgreSqlRoleResource`) — a PostgreSQL login role.\\n- **Firewall rule** (`CosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRuleResource`) — an IP-range rule allowing external access to the cluster.\\n- **Private link resource** (`CosmosDBForPostgreSqlPrivateLinkResource`) — details about private endpoint connections.\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nReplace the Examples section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following examples show common operations. Replace ``, ``, and `` with your own values.\\n\\n### Create a Cluster\\n\\n```\\nArmClient armClient = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\nSubscriptionResource subscription = await armClient.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\nResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\nCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterCollection clusters = resourceGroup.GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters();\\n\\nCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterData clusterData = new CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterData(AzureLocation.EastUS)\\n{\\n    CoordinatorVCores = 4,\\n    CoordinatorStorageQuotaInMb = 524288,\\n    NodeCount = 2,\\n    NodeVCores = 4,\\n    NodeStorageQuotaInMb = 524288,\\n    AdministratorLoginPassword = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n};\\n\\nArmOperation lro = await clusters.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n    WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, clusterData);\\nCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster = lro.Value;\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Cluster ID: {cluster.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n### List Clusters in a Resource Group\\n\\n```\\nArmClient armClient = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\nSubscriptionResource subscription = await armClient.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\nResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\nawait foreach (CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster in resourceGroup.GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters())\\n{\\n    Console.WriteLine(cluster.Data.Name);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Add a Firewall Rule\\n\\n```\\nArmClient armClient = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\nSubscriptionResource subscription = await armClient.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\nResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster = await resourceGroup\\n    .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters()\\n    .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\nCosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRuleData firewallData = new CosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRuleData(\\n    IPAddress.Parse(\\u0026#34;0.0.0.0\\u0026#34;), IPAddress.Parse(\\u0026#34;255.255.255.255\\u0026#34;));\\n\\nArmOperation lro = await cluster\\n    .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRules()\\n    .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;AllowAll\\u0026#34;, firewallData);\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Firewall rule created: {lro.Value.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new test file at `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/tests/Samples/Sample1_Clusters.cs` containing the three snippet methods above, each wrapped in `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` and decorated with `[Test]`.\\n\\nSnippet names to use (must match README placeholders):\\n- `CosmosDBForPostgreSql_CreateCluster`\\n- `CosmosDBForPostgreSql_ListClusters`\\n- `CosmosDBForPostgreSql_AddFirewallRule`\\n\\nExample test file structure:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_Clusters\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateCluster()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CosmosDBForPostgreSql_CreateCluster\\n            ArmClient armClient = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await armClient.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterCollection clusters = resourceGroup.GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters();\\n\\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterData clusterData = new CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterData(AzureLocation.EastUS)\\n            {\\n                CoordinatorVCores = 4,\\n                CoordinatorStorageQuotaInMb = 524288,\\n                NodeCount = 2,\\n                NodeVCores = 4,\\n                NodeStorageQuotaInMb = 524288,\\n                AdministratorLoginPassword = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation lro = await clusters.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, clusterData);\\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Cluster ID: {cluster.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n (CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster in resourceGroup.GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine(cluster.Data.Name);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task AddFirewallRule()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CosmosDBForPostgreSql_AddFirewallRule\\n            ArmClient armClient = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await armClient.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster = await resourceGroup\\n                .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRuleData firewallData = new CosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRuleData(\\n                IPAddress.Parse(\\u0026#34;0.0.0.0\\u0026#34;), IPAddress.Parse(\\u0026#34;255.255.255.255\\u0026#34;));\\n\\n            ArmOperation lro = await cluster\\n                .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRules()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;AllowAll\\u0026#34;, firewallData);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Firewall rule created: {lro.Value.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n it does not describe CosmosDB for PostgreSQL resources (Cluster, Server, FirewallRule, Configuration, CoordinatorConfiguration, NodeConfiguration, PrivateEndpointConnection, Role)\\n- **No code examples in README**: The \\\"Examples\\\" section links only to a generic Azure SDK samples page; no inline code snippets showing how to create a cluster, list servers, manage firewall rules, or update configurations\\n- **No test-backed snippets**: There is no `tests/Samples/` directory and no `#region Snippet:` tags anywhere in the package — the snippet tooling (`Update-Snippets.ps1`) cannot inject any examples\\n- **CHANGELOG 1.1.0-beta.3 is empty**: The upcoming unreleased version section has blank `Features Added`, `Breaking Changes`, `Bugs Fixed`, and `Other Changes` entries even though the TypeSpec migration may have introduced API surface changes\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File: `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** the README to replace the generic sections with service-specific content.\\n\\nReplace the generic \\\"Key concepts\\\" section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql` library provides resource management for [Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted), a managed service for running PostgreSQL workloads at scale.\\n\\n### Resource hierarchy\\n\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlCluster** — The top-level resource representing a cluster (a group of nodes). Create, update, restart, or delete a cluster and promote replicas to independent read-write clusters.\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterServer** — Represents an individual server (coordinator or worker) within a cluster.\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRule** — Manages firewall rules that control external access to the cluster.\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlConfiguration** — A cluster-level configuration parameter applying to all nodes.\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlCoordinatorConfiguration** — A configuration parameter scoped to the coordinator node.\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlNodeConfiguration** — A configuration parameter scoped to worker nodes.\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlPrivateEndpointConnection** — Manages private endpoint connections for the cluster.\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlRole** — Manages database roles (users) within the cluster.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the generic \\\"Examples\\\" section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following sections show code samples for common management scenarios.\\n\\n### Create a cluster\\n\\n```C# Snippet:CreateCosmosDBForPostgreSqlCluster\\n```\\n\\n### List all clusters in a subscription\\n\\n```C# Snippet:ListCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters\\n```\\n\\n### Create a firewall rule\\n\\n```C# Snippet:CreateCosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRule\\n```\\n\\n### Update a configuration parameter\\n\\n```C# Snippet:UpdateCosmosDBForPostgreSqlConfiguration\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate the file `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/tests/Samples/Sample1_Clusters.cs` with NUnit test methods that wrap sample code in `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` tags.\\n\\n**File to create:** `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/tests/Samples/Sample1_Clusters.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation.
